title: Fix Refresh Button, Active Count Sync, Set Price, and Admin Login

requirements.text: Rewrite the Refresh button handler in AdminPage.tsx to forcefully invalidate and re-fetch all application data from the backend (bypassing any cache). The refetch must use React Query's refetch with staleTime=0 and must work correctly on all mobile browsers including Chrome on OnePlus/Android devices. After clicking Refresh, the application list must visibly update with the latest rows from the Applications table.

quotes: Force Refresh Logic: Rewrite the 'Refresh' button code to forcefully re-fetch the latest rows from the Applications table. It must work on all mobile browsers, including phone
Update the API sync code so the Refresh button actually pulls data from the server.

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Refresh on an OnePlus/Android device triggers a network call to the backend and returns updated data.
The application list updates visually after each Refresh click without requiring a page reload.
Refresh does not use stale cached data — it always fetches fresh data from the server.
The button is not disabled or throttled in a way that prevents repeated refreshes on mobile.

requirements.text: Fix the active submission count display in the AdminPage so that after clicking Refresh, the count immediately reflects the real number of submitted applications instead of showing '00'. The count must be derived directly from the freshly fetched application data.

quotes: Sync Active Count: Clicking refresh must immediately change the '00' to the real number of submissions.

acceptanceCriteria: Before any refresh, the count may show 0 as a loading state, but after Refresh completes, it shows the real number of applications.
The count does not remain at '00' after a successful data fetch.
The count is computed from the live fetched list, not a hardcoded or stale value.

requirements.text: Fix the Set Price mutation in useQueries.ts and AdminPage.tsx so that submitting a fee (e.g., 200) does not return an 'Unauthorized' error. The admin actor must be properly authenticated before calling the setFee backend function. After a successful Set Price call, the updated fee must be immediately visible to customers on the DashboardPage without requiring a page reload.

quotes: Set Price Fix: Ensure that when I click 'Set Price', the 200 amount is saved and visible to customers immediately. Remove any 'Unauthorized' errors.

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Set Price' with a value of 200 does not produce an 'Unauthorized' error.
The fee is persisted in the backend and returned in subsequent application fetches.
Customers viewing the DashboardPage see the updated fee amount immediately after the admin saves it.
The admin actor used for the setFee call carries a valid identity/session.

requirements.text: Ensure the admin login credentials vijay@123 (username: vijay, password: 123) and vijay@2026 (username: vijay, password: 2026) both continue to work in the useAdminAuth.ts hook. Do not remove or alter these hardcoded credentials. The 24-hour session stored in localStorage must remain intact.

quotes: Admin Login: Keep vijay@123 and vijay@2026 as the working login.

acceptanceCriteria: Logging in with username 'vijay' and password '123' succeeds and grants admin access.
Logging in with username 'vijay' and password '2026' succeeds and grants admin access.
Invalid credentials still correctly reject login.
Session persists for 24 hours in localStorage.

constraints: Do not change the hardcoded admin credentials vijay@123 and vijay@2026 in useAdminAuth.ts.
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Refresh logic must work on mobile browsers (Android/Chrome/OnePlus) — avoid desktop-only APIs.
nonGoals: Adding new authentication systems or replacing Internet Identity.
Redesigning the admin dashboard UI layout.
Adding new application fields or backend schema changes unrelated to these fixes.
